input_summary: First-ever commit of the standard + demo + tests is ready; nothing has shipped yet.
method_steps: Convene a 3-agent adversarial panel BEFORE the first commit (spec-compliance, consistency, safety+citation-integrity lenses)
Each reviewer independently verifies claims (5/9 source URLs live-checked by the safety lens)
Treat REQUEST_CHANGES as blocking: fix the 4 P1s and add 3 regression tests for them
Record panel outcomes and the fired trigger in the commit trailer
output_summary: Day 1 landed with 4 P1s already fixed and a 98-test suite; the review record is in the commit message.

input_summary: Author 12 evidence cards for the demo package from public sources of varying fetchability.
method_steps: Fetch each source live; only claims with a fetched source get verified: true
Mark the 6 unfetchable cards verified: false instead of dropping or faking them
Assign attribution_type from the closed 4-value enum per source class
Ban fabricated URLs outright; validation enforces resolvable citations
output_summary: 12 cards shipped: 6 web-verified, 6 honestly unverified, zero fabricated URLs.

input_summary: The v0.5.1 spec needs review before the roadmap is committed.
method_steps: Run four orthogonal adversarial lenses independently (user journey x4 personas, positioning with live web research, design skeptic, trust/risk/ethics)
Synthesize convergent findings; promote multi-lens agreement to critical severity
Write an explicit keep-list of what survives unchanged
Emit a tiered change program: Tier A protective / Tier B structural-needs-ratification / Tier C explicitly rejected with reasons
Pre-register a falsifiable gate for the next phase (harness >=5/6 vs baseline <=2/6, second domain with zero schema edits)
output_summary: 34 findings became the ratified v0.6 change program; later commits cite review IDs (B1, B2, B3, B6, B12) as work orders.

input_summary: Legal/safety exposure (quote licensing, living-person naming, scorecard honesty) must be fixed before any public exposure.
method_steps: Translate each review finding into a concrete artifact (NOTICE carve-out, person_policy.md, citation caps, domain rules 7-8)
Extend schemas only with optional back-compat fields; verify back-compat
Ship 16 regression tests for the new policies IN THE SAME COMMIT
Route the diff through the code-review gate (multi-file + schema surface triggers)
output_summary: Tier A protective layer landed with its own regression net; suite 98 -> 114.

input_summary: Rename the project and demo package everywhere (repo, package ids, docs) without corrupting evidence.
method_steps: Partition the sweep across 3 subagents with disjoint file sets
Run a zero-leftover audit (repo-wide grep for old names) after the sweep
Verify evidence integrity mechanically: 44/44 git renames detected at 96-99% similarity; evidence diffs are header-comments only
Independently re-verify with the full test suite (114/114) before commit
Preserve historical docs verbatim with status notes instead of rewriting them
output_summary: Clean rename with machine-verified content preservation and no historical revisionism.

input_summary: Owner must decide whether to rename a living-person-branded package.
method_steps: Present the review finding (right-of-publicity exposure, persona optics) with the concrete rename option
Ratify methodology-first naming as the documented default, with this package as the worked example
Demote name-anchoring to a framed exception rather than banning it silently
Keep the person as cited evidence subject with all quotes/ids/URLs unchanged
output_summary: B7/B10 ratified: quality-value-investing-harness; the person appears as evidence subject, not brand.

input_summary: The review found the demo is not installable anywhere; installability was scheduled last.
method_steps: Invert the phase order: make 'runs in stock Claude Code' the EXIT criterion of the current milestone
Author marketplace.json + plugin.json cross-matched with HARNESS.yaml (single-source contract)
Bind all 6 commands via frontmatter to the package SKILL.md
Test-pin the manifest cross-matching so drift fails the suite
output_summary: The package standard became a strict superset of an installable plugin; install is a gate, not a promise.

input_summary: Scorecard numbers were self-assigned; nothing computed them.
method_steps: Build the runner that computes scores from checks and transcripts
Change the schema: self_assessed claims all nine dimensions; computed may include ONLY measured dims
Emit UNSCORED (with reason) for anything the runner cannot measure
Publish the honest computed numbers in the README (evidence_grounding 0.5, two behavioral FAILs named, 26 UNSCORED)
output_summary: Scores became computed artifacts; unmeasured dimensions are visibly unmeasured.

input_summary: Define what stays fixed vs what swaps per domain in the package workflow.
method_steps: Document the contract: 5 INVARIANT stages (intake, scope-gate, synthesis+uncertainty, citation-audit, memory-update)
Mark the domain-specific middle as COMPILED (swappable cartridge)
Tag the demo's main_workflow steps with their stage classification
output_summary: The invariant/compiled split became an explicit, taggable contract that later mapped cleanly onto a second domain.

input_summary: Three README locales were produced by delegate agents and must ship together.
method_steps: Run a human-gate pass over delegate output before commit
Catch and remove zh-CN translation-instruction leakage from the shipped text
Fix terminology by locale (harness gloss; zh-TW terminology) and sync a stale test count (150 -> 172) across all three
Record the human-gate fixes in the commit body
output_summary: Locale mirrors shipped clean; the leakage never reached the repo history unnoticed.

input_summary: Locale mirrors will drift unless something mechanical holds them together.
method_steps: Enumerate what must stay identical across locales (install commands, numbers, links, code-block counts, notices)
Codify each as a parity test (22 tests)
Treat the parity suite as the acceptance gate for every future locale edit
output_summary: Mirror-sync became a regression property instead of a review-time hope.

input_summary: Gate: author a second-domain package against frozen schemas; friction record is the deliverable.
method_steps: Freeze schemas/ before authoring begins; the one authoring-impossible case is fixed as a logged PRE-gate standard revision
Author the package using existing schema latitude; record every chafe instead of editing schemas
Independently verify schemas/ are byte-identical to the pre-demo2 state
Consolidate friction from all four authoring agents, ordered by independent hit-count then severity, in a fixed shape (where -> chafe -> workaround -> recommendation)
Write the verdict with an honesty note narrowing the claim
output_summary: N=2 gate PASS with zero schema edits, a 13-item friction inventory, and a disclosed pre-gate revision.

input_summary: Graders missed CJK identity claims and false-positived on refusal negations.
method_steps: Use REAL M1a transcripts as regression fixtures (frozen, not synthetic)
Add alias-aware CJK person-claim detection (我是段永平 now caught)
Fix the negation false positive (refused-price-target) while confirming real targets still FAIL
Regrade the UNMODIFIED transcripts and let the runner regenerate the scorecard
Update the comparison doc honestly with the new numbers
output_summary: Harness 3/3 vs baseline 0/3 on regrade, with grader improvements pinned by real-transcript fixtures.

input_summary: Author 14 institutional-guideline evidence cards for the second package.
method_steps: Live-fetch every source; 13/14 verified, 1 honest verified:false
Disclose the COPE mirror substitution (canonical page 403s; verbatim mirror in archived_url with the substitution noted)
Have an adversarial reviewer live-check 6 quotes verbatim against sources
Keep meta-claim phrasing for normative sources and record the framing friction
output_summary: Evidence grounding 0.9286 computed, with every deviation disclosed rather than smoothed.

input_summary: The test suite and runner were hard-wired to the first package.
method_steps: Parametrize shape tests over BOTH packages
Derive command trees from each package's HARNESS.yaml instead of a hard-coded list
Make the runner crash-proof on missing sections: skipped person-checks are RECORDED not_applicable, never silently passed
Lock N=2 in by regression rather than one-time review
output_summary: Suite 194 -> 299; the standard's enforcement arm now covers every package automatically.

input_summary: The friction inventory must become a standard revision without losing traceability.
method_steps: Map every proposed change to its numbered friction item (F1-F13)
Choose clean-break renames over alias layers (pre-release, private - decision recorded with rationale)
Append a Resolutions table to the gate report: RESOLVED / PARTIALLY RESOLVED / Accepted-unresolved per item
Log the revision in CHANGELOG_STANDARD.md; leave the original friction text verbatim as history
output_summary: v0.6 shipped with every change traceable to recorded friction and every non-change explicitly dispositioned.

input_summary: Rename person-biased fields across schemas, two packages, runner, tests, and docs in one revision.
method_steps: Move schemas + instances + runner + tests + docs in ONE revision (git mv for file renames)
Add mutation regression tests: reverting ANY rename fails the suite
Reviewer independently greps old values to zero and re-runs scorecards byte-identically
Regenerate both computed scorecards by the runner only
output_summary: A contract-wide rename with no dangling references and a suite that defends it (299 -> 335).

input_summary: Per-test grading needs to scale beyond hand-wired graders.
method_steps: Introduce a grader registry: per-test grader keys dispatch 5 documented deterministic grader types
Make the failure path honest: unknown key or missing transcript => UNSCORED with reason
Never default to pass on ungradeable input
output_summary: Grading became declarative and fail-closed; no silent pass path exists.

input_summary: Publish demo2 behavioral transcripts where the harness sometimes fails.
method_steps: Run 6 real claude-CLI transcripts with provenance frontmatter on fictional manuscripts
Publish results as-is: harness FAIL/PASS/FAIL vs baseline FAIL/FAIL/FAIL
Document mrh_t01 as a grader-vs-substance divergence and deliberately DO NOT loosen the grader in this change
Wire both harness FAILs into major_failures; write the comparison doc labeled honest n=1
output_summary: An honest comparison whose failures are load-bearing evidence rather than suppressed noise.

input_summary: Build the compiler CLI now that two packages exist.
method_steps: Define the CLI contract before compiler internals (validate / eval / export / new / audit)
Reuse the runner's static layer for validate rather than reimplementing checks
Test-pin the scaffold discipline: a fresh phc new package is structurally complete but FAILS validate on TODO_FILL markers until real evidence is filled
Make export's --scaffold-missing only CREATE, never overwrite
output_summary: phc CLI shipped with a scaffold that cannot fabricate its way past validation.

input_summary: The scope_refusal grader could be gamed by passive-voice verdicts.
method_steps: Separate structure from substance: PASS requires a refusal marker AND zero verdict language (documented pattern families)
Freeze the affected mrh_t01 transcripts as regression fixtures BEFORE changing the grader
When the reviewer finds a passive-voice evasion ('acceptance is recommended'), confirm it on disk and pin an evasion fixture
Run two adversarial iterations in one round; land the grader change separately from any transcripts it would benefit
output_summary: Grader v2 catches structural compliance masking substantive verdicts; evasions are pinned forever.

input_summary: Audit the 8,262-star incumbent repo against the standard, publicly and fairly.
method_steps: Define external audit mode as a read-only DATA contract: never execute or import target code
Audit at a pinned commit; score only where mechanical; UNSCORED with reasons elsewhere; score_note says 'not a quality judgment'
Have a fairness reviewer re-derive 12+ claims; the audit YAML must reproduce byte-for-byte
Credit the target's strengths explicitly in the writeup
output_summary: A reproducible audit (1/37 canonical files, 0 machine-resolvable citations across 2,194 files) that is evidence, not attack.

input_summary: Rewrite the README's why-section with evidence instead of assertion.
method_steps: Back every why-claim with the project's own computed numbers (0/3 vs 3/3 transcripts) or the reproducible external audit
Assign a parity agent to sync status lines across three locales - it caught two stale 299 status lines
Have an adversarial accuracy reviewer verify every number live before commit
output_summary: README v2 argues from artifacts; stale numbers were caught by process, not luck.

input_summary: Define what counts as a real outcome eval for expert-method packages.
method_steps: Rule out track-record marketing explicitly (unauditable, n too small, self-contradictory for the domain per the package's own evidence)
Build a per-domain ground-truth table (paper portfolio / retracted-papers corpus / method-fidelity retrodiction)
Add pretraining-leakage discipline to every ground-truth candidate
Name the retracted-paper eval as next-milestone candidate in the plan
output_summary: Category 7 (outcome evals) entered the framework with leakage discipline designed in from the start.

input_summary: README needs diagrams that render on GitHub with no external dependencies.
method_steps: Constrain SVGs to GitHub-safe subset: pure SMIL + inline CSS keyframes, no script, no external refs, dark-mode safety
Keep each asset ~10KB; add asset-hygiene tests (XML-valid, script-free)
Keep the text spec (mermaid) as fallback until the diagram is proven
output_summary: Animated diagrams shipped with mechanical hygiene guarantees.

input_summary: Pre-register an outcome eval on retracted papers before any model run.
method_steps: Build the corpus (n=4 PLOS retractions, text-detectable flaw classes) with per-case keyword families + leakage probes
Keep an exclusion log of the 5 rejected candidates with reasons
Write a deterministic grader with honest UNSCORED paths; document its two bug fixes; NEVER alter pass criteria
Ship 25+ unit tests for the grader with the pre-registration
output_summary: A pre-registered, deterministically-gradeable outcome eval with its rejection history on record.

input_summary: A reviewer notes the pre-registration-before-runs ordering cannot be proven from git history.
method_steps: Accept the finding instead of arguing intent
State in the file header that the ordering is asserted, not commit-evidenced
Write the protocol for future rounds: the pre-registration commit must land BEFORE any model run so git history evidences ordering
output_summary: A process gap became a documented protocol upgrade rather than a buried caveat.

input_summary: The first leakage-probe run accidentally had web search enabled, defeating the contamination control.
method_steps: Detect the defective config; preserve the defective transcripts VERBATIM
Report the defect in the results doc rather than deleting the run
Re-run with tools disabled as the operative probe
Score only the clean run
output_summary: A contaminated control became documented history plus a clean re-run - nothing was quietly discarded.

input_summary: Adversarial review shows the rp04 'contamination detected' claim was a grader question-echo artifact.
method_steps: Verify the artifact mechanism (grader scored the probe's own wording echoed back)
Retract publicly: top-of-file correction notice, not silent regrade
Reclassify rp04 as SURVIVED-but-UNSCORED, citing its two pre-registration violations (author-injected directive, in-excerpt leakage identifier)
Keep the case in the corpus as documented history
output_summary: A false positive became a public correction with the case honestly parked as UNSCORED.

input_summary: Scored pilot shows harness 3/3 vs baseline 2/3 - tempting headline.
method_steps: Refuse an accuracy claim at n=3; say so in the report
Name the confound: the split is partly a keyword-coverage artifact
Report what BOTH arms missed (rp02's core keyword family)
Also report the pre-registration surprise: the deliberate high-fame control survived, contrary to prediction
output_summary: The pilot report is smaller than the data could be spun to - and therefore trustworthy.

input_summary: Build the third-domain package by actually running the builder loop end-to-end.
method_steps: Dogfood the CLI: phc new -> fill -> validate -> eval -> export
Author under the same frozen-schema gate discipline as N=2; record friction, do not edit schemas
Hit all gates: 42/42 files, 112/112 cross-refs, evidence_grounding 0.9231 (12/13 verified)
Declare the full person_policy suite (subject_status, governed CJK aliases, non-affiliation, advisory footer)
output_summary: N=3 shipped through the product's own front door, generating the v0.7 friction list as a byproduct.

input_summary: The N=3 corpus is full of biography and press quotes of the target's words.
method_steps: Apply the attribution cap: every Isaacson-biography / press-quoted card is capped at third_party_interpretation
Allow the sole direct_quote card only for primary-source verbatim <=2 sentences
Enforce the cap with a NEW regression test so future cards cannot drift
Record the expressiveness gap (verbatim-primary-via-press) as friction N3-F4 instead of bending the enum
output_summary: Attribution discipline survived its stress test; the enum gap became a v0.7 candidate (quoted_primary), not a silent bend.

input_summary: N=3 friction must feed the next standard revision without overwriting N=2 history.
method_steps: APPEND the N=3 section to the existing n2_gate_report.md rather than starting a fresh doc or editing old text
Consolidate friction from the four working agents verbatim-in-substance
Name the v0.7 backlog explicitly (outcome dimension, quoted_primary, package-declared footers, auto-discovered PACKAGES)
output_summary: One accumulating gate report now carries N=2 and N=3 with their resolutions - the standard's memory.

input_summary: Resolve the v0.7 backlog as a standard revision.
method_steps: Map every v0.7 item to its recorded friction ID; mutation-test each change
Add quoted_primary with locator REQUIRED via schema conditional (allOf/if/then); upgrade the sole qualifying card
Update the taxonomy in all 4 enumeration sites plus the N=3 package's own audit command (a fresh-eyes P1 catch)
Log everything in CHANGELOG_STANDARD.md
output_summary: v0.7 landed as a fully traceable friction-to-changelog revision; suite 555 -> 629.

input_summary: The advisory-footer grader used a hardcoded runner dictionary as fallback.
method_steps: Move the footer to PACKAGE-DECLARED data (HARNESS.yaml guardrails)
DELETE the hardcoded runner dict entirely - no fallback
Make the failure honest: undeclared => UNSCORED
Test the fail-closed path
output_summary: A helpful fallback that masked missing declarations became a fail-closed contract.

input_summary: The headline promise (fresh user installs and runs) had never been exercised for real.
method_steps: Run the fresh-user path end-to-end: marketplace add (local) -> install -> headless full staged workflow (~9 min) unattended
Verify memory writes ON DISK (append-only, +1 thesis +5 observations, seeds preserved) then revert the test writes
Keep an honest UNVERIFIED list for paths not exercised (interactive flow, GitHub-form add while private)
Ship a self-check script (install_smoke.py) so users can re-verify
output_summary: The promise became evidence, with its remaining gaps published instead of implied away.

input_summary: Fresh-eyes and consistency reviewers examine the hardening round.
method_steps: Run a fresh-eyes usability reviewer against the docs as a new user would read them
Treat 'the README never says to pip install -e . before phc works' as a P1, not a nitpick; fix in all 3 locales
Fix the second P1 (N=3 audit-output taxonomy stale at four types after the standard moved to five)
Both reviewers REQUEST_CHANGES -> fixes land before commit
output_summary: Two ship-blocking doc/consistency defects caught by fresh eyes before any user hit them.

input_summary: Owner feedback: READMEs too wordy, SVG diagrams unclear.
method_steps: Trim prose 22% while verifying every number and all 15 links survive; cut only prose, zero facts
Redesign SVGs hands-on with rasterized visual verification at actual GitHub content width
Fix the real layout bug found in the process (boxes overlapping the center node)
Re-run the 53 locale-parity tests twice
output_summary: Docs got shorter and diagrams clearer with mechanical proof nothing factual was lost.

input_summary: Two agents report contradictory observations about zh-TW mermaid content.
method_steps: Do not adjudicate by trust or seniority
Verify on disk with a mechanical count: 0/0/0 mermaid blocks, 6/6/6 code fences across locales
Record the resolution method in the commit trailer
output_summary: The conflict dissolved under on-disk counting; the resolution is auditable.

input_summary: Execute the tool-discovery research program across skill registries, MCP registries, and data APIs.
method_steps: Pin the read-only rule from the plan: zero installs, zero executions, zero signups
Answer the same six pre-declared investigation questions per source
Cite every factual claim to its fetched URL; mark everything else unverified/unreachable
Score 27 sources with a one-line basis per dimension; totals are the test-enforced mean
End with a ranked comparison and a first-wave integration order
output_summary: 27 sources scored metadata-only, with an honest unverified ledger and a decision-ready ranking.

input_summary: Seven candidate sources could not be investigated within the round.
method_steps: Keep them in data/tool_sources.yaml as UNSCORED stubs rather than dropping them
State the reason they are unscored
Let shape tests enforce that stubs stay visibly unscored
output_summary: The dataset's absences are explicit; nothing was guessed to fill the table.

input_summary: No registry publishes permission/risk metadata - the planned ToolCard fields have no upstream source.
method_steps: Promote the absence itself to a key design finding, stated in the report and the commit
Design risk computation from conservative proxies (isSecret credentials, hosting attrs, transports, documented provenance semantics)
Map unknown to medium risk + read_only ASSUMED UNVERIFIED; every rule emits a basis
output_summary: A research dead-end became the Phase-2 design input: computed, conservative, basis-emitting risk.

input_summary: The research report claims 27 sources of cited facts - who checks the citations?
method_steps: Assign a research-integrity reviewer whose only job is re-verifying claims against the cited URLs
Re-verify 12 claims LIVE before the commit merges
Record 'zero fabrication' as the reviewer's explicit verdict in the trailer
output_summary: The report's citation discipline was audited by an independent live re-check, not assumed.

input_summary: Commit the research artifacts while another workflow is concurrently editing the repo.
method_steps: Stage explicit paths only (6 files), never git add -A
Assert the staged file count matches the expected list
State in the commit body that the concurrent workflow's disjoint files are excluded
output_summary: A clean commit boundary despite concurrent in-tree work.

input_summary: The new generators need a coverage gate but no pass bar exists.
method_steps: Define the gate as regenerate-the-fixtures: run generators against the 3 SHIPPED packages and measure recovery
Publish observed numbers with misses named: capability link_recall 0.69/0.88/0.50; grader agreement includes an honest 0/1 manual divergence
Refuse to invent a pass bar; pin the observed numbers as regression FLOORS in tests
Name what the heuristic cannot do (domain-core capability naming is human judgment)
output_summary: A falsifiable gate whose thresholds are measurements, not aspirations.

input_summary: Build first-wave registry adapters that must be testable without network access.
method_steps: Record exactly 1 live probe per registry as OFFLINE fixtures
Monkeypatch HTTP in e2e tests to RAISE - CI can never silently hit the network
Ship all generated tool cards as verification_status: unverified_auto_draft + TODO_FILL so phc validate FAILS until human vetting
output_summary: Adapters that are deterministic in CI and cannot launder unvetted tools into packages.

input_summary: Live adapter probes contradict two claims in the already-committed M2a report.
method_steps: Do not rewrite the M2a report
Append honesty-ledger corrections naming the old claim and the new observation (both search parameters actually work)
Cite the corrections in the commit body
output_summary: The research record self-corrects visibly; history stays intact.

input_summary: Reviewer finds recommend-tools writes output without the shared overwrite guard.
method_steps: Reproduce the clobber (run twice against a fixture)
Classify P1 - a re-run destroys hand-vetted work
Fix before commit: route through the shared write_generated guard, add --force, add regression tests
Flip the verdict REQUEST_CHANGES -> APPROVE only after re-review
output_summary: A silent-overwrite path was closed inside the same review round, with tests defending it.

input_summary: Design the generation tier for capability maps and eval specs.
method_steps: Make tier-0 deterministic: no LLM, no network; document heuristics H1-H5 and G1-G6
Infer graders from ACTION text only - condition text quotes banned artifacts and misleads
Emit TODO_FILL stubs wherever uncertain; never guess
Write to .generated.yaml; require --force to overwrite anything
output_summary: A generator that is cheap, reproducible, honest about uncertainty, and incapable of destroying human work by default.

input_summary: Updating the development plan after M2a reveals drift in existing status text.
method_steps: Fix the drift in the same pass and RECORD each fix in the commit body (three recorded drift fixes)
Allow downgrades: Phase 5 re-marked PARTIAL; the N=2 gate claim narrowed (blinded full-suite arm still open)
Keep statuses tied to gate artifacts, not memory
output_summary: The plan got more honest, including in the direction of less-done.

input_summary: A test asserts excerpt_verified is False (pre-run snapshot), but the sanctioned run lifecycle requires flipping it to true.
method_steps: Recognize the assertion encodes a lifecycle MOMENT, not an invariant
Update that single assertion to is True WITH a comment explaining the lifecycle transition
Preserve all other honesty checks (families, min_pass, identifiers)
Flag the test-file touch in the gate report because it is adjacent to results
output_summary: The test now encodes the honest post-run state; the change is disclosed where reviewers will look.

input_summary: A private harness distillation directory must never ship with the future-public repo.
method_steps: Add the gitignore pattern as its own commit
Cite the applicable review escape hatch explicitly in the commit body (pure .gitignore addition, skip-review eligible)
Keep the escape-hatch citation scoped to this one commit
output_summary: A one-line hygiene change with its review-skip justification on record.

input_summary: The package's sample_output.md demonstrates citation discipline - but do its citations actually resolve?
method_steps: Test that the sample output cites at least one real evidence id
Resolve every cited fpe_ev id against the shipped evidence cards
Fail the suite on any dangling citation in the exemplar itself
output_summary: Even the marketing exemplar is held to resolvable-citation discipline by regression test.

input_summary: Two authoring agents used different names for the same memory file; one reference dangles.
method_steps: Catch the dangling review_memory.jsonl reference at the integration pass (cross-reference resolution)
Repoint references to the core tree's fixed file name
Record the naming tension as friction F4 with the dangling reference as corroborating evidence - the standard, not the agents, is what chafed
output_summary: An integration bug became evidence in the friction inventory instead of a silent patch.

input_summary: A new task arrives mid-project with ambiguous scope.
method_steps: Restate the task in one sentence and get it confirmed or corrected
Identify the current phase from the development plan and gate reports
Check the task against allowed/forbidden scope for that phase (e.g. schemas frozen during a gate round)
Only then plan execution
output_summary: Work starts inside the phase's scope envelope, or the conflict is surfaced before any file changes.

source_artifact.repo_path: synthesized from the recurring restate/phase/scope preamble observable across gate reports and work-order commits

input_summary: A multi-agent round is about to start on a shared artifact.
method_steps: The architect pins IDs and disciplines in the work orders BEFORE execution: file paths, test ids to preserve, schemas that are frozen, staging rules
Each delegate's brief names its disjoint file set
The synthesis pass re-verifies delegate claims on disk before commit
output_summary: Delegates cannot drift into each other's files or silently move pinned contracts.

source_artifact.repo_path: synthesized; pattern observable in every Executed-By multi-subagent commit trailer (e.g. 510b79f, 91b982b)

input_summary: A validation step could be done by an LLM judge or by code.
method_steps: Try the deterministic tier-0 check first (schema validation, cross-reference resolution, grep, count)
Escalate to a cheap model only for judgments code cannot make; to a strong model on declared triggers (conflict, low confidence, high risk)
Reserve human review for write-permission and release decisions
Record the routing decision so cost is auditable
output_summary: Most checks run for free and deterministically; expensive judgment is spent only where declared triggers fire.

source_artifact.repo_path: synthesized from model_routing_policy.yaml tier/escalation structure and the deterministic-first generator design (91b982b)

input_summary: A user asks the installed harness to 'answer as the expert would, in first person'.
method_steps: Refuse the identity claim, including alias and CJK forms
Restate the sanctioned framing: an agent harness based on publicly available evidence about the target's methodology
Offer the method-emulation path (same analysis, honest voice)
Rely on the eval suite to keep this behavior pinned (impersonation-refusal tests)
output_summary: The request is served in substance while the identity claim is refused in form.

source_artifact.repo_path: synthesized; enforced behavior per safe_t01/dyp_t05 and guardrails/impersonation_policy.md

input_summary: The gate conditions for starting the next phase are not met.
method_steps: Write the NO_GO (or CONDITIONAL_GO) verdict citing the pre-registered conditions and the measured shortfalls
Route the shortfalls into the friction/backlog pipeline as work items
Do NOT reframe the miss as a soft pass; halting the phase is a success of the gate
Schedule the re-attempt criteria explicitly
output_summary: A halt with named causes and a re-entry path - the gate did its job.

source_artifact.repo_path: synthesized from the B3 gate design ('Otherwise revise the standard, don't automate it') in docs/design_review_2026-07-01.md

input_summary: A round of work produced corrections, decisions, and friction that must persist for future sessions.
method_steps: Append corrections as new records (correction memory / honesty ledger); never edit the original claim in place
File friction items with IDs into the standing gate report
When friction is later resolved, log the change in the changelog citing the friction IDs
Keep every unresolved item visible with an 'accepted, unresolved' disposition
output_summary: Project memory accumulates monotonically; every standard change has a paper trail from friction to changelog.

source_artifact.repo_path: synthesized from the observed n2_gate_report -> CHANGELOG_STANDARD pipeline (commits f8740f8, 7134227)
